content:
## Page Section / Title
Meerkat  
THE MAIN CHARACTER  


## All Verbatim Text
- "AISALON Brand Guidelines | by Sightbox"  
- "1.1"  
- "Meerkat"  
- "THE MAIN CHARACTER"  
- "The Meerkat is the character with which users worldwide identify. Its distinctive design symbolizes connection and innovation, making it a key part of the AISALON experience."  


## Visual Elements
- **Logos**: Not applicable (page focuses on character design, not brand logo).  
- **Color Palette**: No explicit color swatches or names/HEX/RGB values shown; colors are part of the meerkat’s geometric design (vibrant, varied hues: oranges, yellows, blues, purples, reds).  
- **Typography**:  
  - Heading: "Meerkat" (bold, sans-serif, large size).  
  - Subheading: "THE MAIN CHARACTER" (uppercase, sans-serif, smaller than heading).  
  - Body text: Regular sans-serif (smaller than subheading).  
- **Imagery / Photography**: Three stylized, low-poly meerkats with geometric, faceted shapes; vibrant, multicolored palettes; clean white background; focus on character design and visual identity.  
- **Iconography**: Not applicable (no icons present).  
- **Layout Grid**: Implied grid with left-aligned text column (≈1/4 page width) and right-aligned image column (≈3/4 page width); consistent spacing between text and images.  
- **Geometric / Decorative Elements**: Low-poly (polygonal) shapes forming the meerkats; sharp, angular edges; gradient-like color transitions within each polygon.  


## Page Layout Summary
Left-aligned text block (section title, subheading, body copy) occupies the left 25% of the page. Three meerkat illustrations are centered in the right 75% of the page, arranged horizontally with equal spacing between them. The layout is clean and minimal, with ample white space around elements.  


## Design Rules / Guidance Visible on Page
- The Meerkat is defined as the "main character" for AISALON, symbolizing connection and innovation.  
- No explicit do/don't rules or usage guidelines are printed on the page; focus is on introducing the character’s role and visual style.
